features.summary: Global Blood Therapeutics reported Q1 2022 revenue of $55.2 million from Oxbryta sales, representing 41% year-over-year growth and meeting guidance of $54-56 million. The company delivered over 1,200 new prescriptions, the strongest demand quarter since Q1 2020, driven by the pediatric launch (ages 4-11) and continued growth in the 12+ population. Sequential revenue declined slightly from Q4 due to inventory normalization and increased gross-to-net adjustments. The company ended Q1 with $662 million in cash and provided Q2 2022 revenue guidance of $63-65 million.
features.management_outlook: Management expects sequential revenue growth of 14-18% in Q2 2022, driven by new prescription growth and phasing effects from Q1. They anticipate roughly flat new prescriptions in Q2 as the initial pediatric bolus moderates but expect continued incremental improvement in the 12+ age group. The company remains optimistic about momentum continuing as COVID-19 headwinds subside and patient visits normalize. For the second half of 2022, management believes there is potential to accelerate growth driven by ongoing DTC campaigns, new real-world data, and the pediatric launch expansion.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID-19 impacts on patient healthcare visits, which remain below pre-pandemic levels especially for younger patients whose parents remain cautious about exposure. The company faces timing uncertainties around broad pediatric payer coverage achievement and potential new COVID variants that could disrupt patient care patterns. Gross-to-net adjustments are expected to increase incrementally to reach 25% by mid-year, and there are operational challenges around European launch execution and reimbursement negotiations in key markets.
features.key_drivers: Primary growth drivers include the successful pediatric launch with strong early payer coverage progress, continued momentum in the 12+ population with March being the highest month for active prescribers in 12 months, and European expansion with Germany launch planned for mid-May. The pipeline advancement of GBT-601 with patient-driven Phase 1 restart at 150mg dose and planned Phase 2/3 initiation by mid-2022 provides significant upside potential. The company's comprehensive DTC campaign continues exceeding reach goals and driving website engagement to all-time highs.
